title: Circuit Zandvoort
sub: A thrilling old-school rollercoaster through the North Sea dunes, modernised with steep banking at Turn 3 and the final corner. Narrow, undulating and notoriously hard to pass on.
mapCredit: Will Pittenger / Wikimedia Commons · CC BY-SA

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The wide Tarzan hairpin (best passing spot), then the steeply banked Turn 3 Hugenholtz that slingshots up into the dunes.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: Blind crests and the fearsome Scheivlak, weaving through the dunes. Undulating, committed, and impossible to follow closely.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The technical run to the dramatically banked final corner, Arie Luyendykbocht, which fires cars onto the main straight at full lean.

tyreAlloc: Middle of the range · C2 / C3 / C4
stratNote: The banking and constant loading wear the tyres hard, and the raised 2025 pit-speed limit made the two-stop more tempting. With passing so hard, strategy and the undercut do a lot of the work.
spots: Wide hairpin at the end of the main straight + DRS. The one genuine, classic overtaking spot.
The banked last corner feeds the DRS straight; a tow can set up a Tarzan move.
Narrow, dune-lined and hard to follow, so the undercut often beats on-track passing.
otNote: Narrow and undulating with little room to run side by side. The banked corners help cars follow a touch closer, but Tarzan is realistically the only true passing place.
corners: Wide 180° right at the end of the main straight + DRS. The best (and main) overtaking spot.
Short right linking to the banked corner.
Steeply banked left that slingshots the car up into the dunes.
Quick uphill right opening the dune section.
Blind, undulating direction changes through the dunes.
A fearsome, committed downhill right over a crest. A highlight of the lap.
Medium right toward the final sector.
Quick left starting the run home.
Technical flick setting up the banked finale.
The dramatically banked final right; cars take it at full lean onto the main straight and DRS.
geo: A seaside circuit threaded through the Dutch coastal dunes, opened 1948. Narrow, undulating and full of blind crests.
The 2021 revamp added IndyCar-style banking at Turn 3 (Hugenholtz) and the final corner, letting cars carry huge speed and follow a touch closer.
Max Verstappen’s home race, with grandstands a sea of orange. He won the first three on its return (2021-23).
Sea breezes swing the balance and blow sand onto the track, lowering grip; the wind direction can change a setup lap to lap.
